last_item: Band-2 (stability): Audit every web API call for explicit error+loading+empty states — close the one silent-failure gap where a failed poll froze stale data without telling the operator.
last_result: done (iter 24) — closed the last open band-2 item: web API silent-failure audit. Every data-fetch surface (useAsync/usePolling consumers + local-state forms across all 7 views) already had loading+error+empty paths EXCEPT one real gap: usePolling (web/src/lib/hooks.ts) retains the last good `data` and only sets `error` on a failed poll, but the three pollers gated error UI on `!data`, so a poll that began FAILING after first load was silently swallowed (Dashboard froze on a stale '● Healthy' badge + 'refreshes every 5s' while unreachable; Migrate DirectJobProgress/SessionProgress kept a spinner/stepper that looked like live progress). Added reusable `StaleBanner` (web/src/components/ui.tsx — warn tone, role=alert, KEEPS cached data visible, says 'Live updates paused — the latest refresh failed: <msg>' + hint), surfaced whenever `error` accompanies cached data: Dashboard always, the two Migrate pollers only while `!terminal` (post-completion blip must not alarm). Tests: 2 StaleBanner unit tests + Dashboard.test.tsx (mocks @/lib/hooks usePolling via vi.hoisted: banner on error+data, hidden on clean poll, full ErrorNotice still on first-load failure). Proven non-vacuous (stripping the Dashboard wiring fails the banner test). Web gate green (typecheck/build/42 tests); Go gates untouched + green (sandbox-disabled). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): caught SessionProgress as a 3rd unprotected poller — fixed before commit; confirmed terminal-gate prevents flicker, error.message is panel-authored (no leak), the two role=alert regions are mutually exclusive. BAND 2 (STABILITY) COMPLETE → next band 2.5. PREVIOUS (iter 23) — provisioning flow was already idempotent (apt install / `systemctl enable --now` no-op when done; provisionSQL guards every stmt with DO/IF NOT EXISTS), but nothing PROVED a re-run is a no-op and Provision always reported 'Postgres provisioned'. Surfaced an honest signal for the ONE genuinely state-mutating step (EnsureSocketAuth → pg_hba.conf, already returns a `changed` bool): Provision result data now carries `socket_auth: configured|already-present` and install.go logs it. Scoped the claim to that step only — message does NOT assert the whole provision was a no-op (apt could have upgraded a package). Added TestProvision_SecondRunIsIdempotentNoOp: two Provision calls share one FakeRunner + one persistent real pg_hba.conf; asserts second run succeeds, managed block appears EXACTLY ONCE (a duplicate would corrupt auth), no extra pg_reload_conf fires, socket_auth flips to already-present. Proven non-vacuous (breaking injectHBARules idempotency fails it). Normal gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled). web/ untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking findings. PREVIOUS (iter 22): added TestReadOnlyPool_StatementTimeoutEnforced to internal/pg/readonly_integration_test.go (//go:build integration, env-gated on INDIEPG_TEST_SOCKET; NEVER runs in the untagged loop gate, like its sibling). Sets a 250ms read-pool cap + 30s client ctx so the cancellation observed is the SERVER statement_timeout, not the Go deadline. (1) Real query-box path: ExecuteRead('SELECT pg_sleep(5)') is cancelled with a 'statement timeout' message in <5s. (2) Raw read-pool conn proves wire-level SQLSTATE 57014 (query_canceled) — ExecuteRead collapses PgError to a string, so the raw conn is needed for the code assertion. (3) Positive control: pg_sleep(0.5) on the PRIV pool COMPLETES, proving the cap is scoped to the read pool only (priv pool deliberately has no forced timeout for long guided maintenance). Auto-LIMIT half was already fully covered (guard Check/injectLimit/EnsureLimit unit tests; server wires AutoLimit: cfg.QueryLimit; handler runs guard.Check before ExecuteRead) — verified, no new test needed. Proven green against a throwaway PG14 cluster provisioned with the indiepg roles (both integration tests pass; my test ~1.0s not 5s+, confirming the cap fired). Normal gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap). web/ untouched. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking findings — confirmed the wrong-reason guard (client ctx vs server timeout) is real via the 30s outer deadline, the 57014 assertion hits the right path via the raw conn, and the positive control is correctly scoped.
notes: BAND 1.5 + BAND 2 (STABILITY) COMPLETE. NOW IN BAND 2.5 (resource & config safety). Band-2.5 open items: (a) disk-headroom alert before data/WAL volume fills (alert rule + test); (b) SELF-HEALING config — a postgresql.conf change (default OR user override) that stops PG auto-rolls-back to last-known-good, PG never left down (test: bad setting → rollback → PG up); (c) host-sized tuning at provision (shared_buffers/work_mem/effective_cache_size/max_connections from detected RAM/CPU per DEFAULTS.md; test the sizing fn against sample RAM); (d) connection-saturation alert as active conns approach max_connections (rule + test). The alert-engine plumbing is already wired (Collector.SampleOnce → Engine.Evaluate → dispatchAlertEvents, background.go; metricValue reads Snapshot fields via alert/metrics.go) — disk/conn alerts likely just need new DefaultRules() + a snapshot metric if not already present (Snapshot already has disk_used/total_bytes, connections, max_connections — so (a)/(d) may be rule-only). DONE iter 24: web silent-failure audit closed — `usePolling` retains last `data` & only sets `error` on a failed poll; the 3 pollers gated error UI on `!data` so a refresh that started failing post-first-load was swallowed. Added StaleBanner (ui.tsx) surfaced on error+cached-data: Dashboard always, Migrate DirectJobProgress/SessionProgress while !terminal. Frontend test pattern for a polling view: vi.mock('@/lib/hooks') with a vi.hoisted() mutable state object, set pollState.current = {data,error,loading,reload} per case (see web/src/views/Dashboard.test.tsx) — no fake timers/fetch needed. DONE iter 23: provisioning idempotent — 2nd Provision proven a safe no-op (TestProvision_SecondRunIsIdempotentNoOp) + reports `socket_auth: configured|already-present` honestly (Provision result data + install.go log; scoped to the one stateful step, EnsureSocketAuth). DONE iter 22: query-box guards verified — auto-LIMIT already covered (guard unit tests + server wiring + handler), and statement_timeout NOW PROVEN ENFORCED at the DB level on the read pool (TestReadOnlyPool_StatementTimeoutEnforced, integration-tagged/env-gated, never runs in the loop gate). Query-box plumbing: guard.Check (internal/pg/guard) classifies+auto-LIMITs before s.pg.ExecuteRead (internal/pg/query.go) on the read pool; read pool's statement_timeout comes from cfg.StatementTimeout via buildDSN (internal/pg/connect.go); priv pool has NO forced timeout by design. To prove an integration test green: stand up throwaway PG14 (initdb -A trust -U postgres; pg_ctl -o "-c listen_addresses='' -c unix_socket_directories=<dir>" start), apply provisionSQL role stmts (indiepg_readonly+indiepg_admin login roles, plus REVOKE CREATE ON SCHEMA public FROM PUBLIC + GRANT to admin for the write-denial test), point INDIEPG_TEST_SOCKET at the socket dir; binaries /usr/lib/postgresql/14/bin; go is a snap so run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; use /tmp not empty $TMPDIR. --- HISTORICAL BAND 1.5 (done): (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced. (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP wired (background.go). (4) SCHEDULED BACKUPS RUN: full+incremental cron jobs; Manager.backupMu single-flight = CodeConflict skip on overlap (no fail row/false alert). (5) off-host S3 nudge test-locked. (6) restore-test SURFACING (restoreTestStatus + RestoreTestStatus banner). (7) ***restore-test EXECUTION DONE: Manager.RestoreTest runs `pgbackrest verify` (VerifyCmd, command.go verifyTimeout=2h); handler wired; recordRestoreTest helper mirrors recordBackup; verify is READ-ONLY so it does NOT take backupMu (correct — won't corrupt). verify ≠ full restore, so verified_rows stays 0 (honest).*** (8) ***restore-test JOB WIRED (iter 18): startBackgroundJobs now registers a `restore-test` cron job on cfg.Schedules.RestoreTest via a generic registerJob helper; scheduledRestoreTest calls Manager.RestoreTest directly and deliberately SKIPS ensureBackupConfigured (its stanza-create exclusive lock + possible PG restart would collide with a long full backup still running at 05:00) — verify is a pure reader.*** (9) ***restore-test DEEP proof CORE DONE (iter 19): Manager.RestoreTestDeep — non-destructive scratch restore (os.MkdirTemp, --pg1-path override ≠ live dir) + pg_ctl boot (full WAL replay, private socket, archive_mode=off) + real row count into verified_rows + guaranteed teardown (deferred stop+RemoveAll). Headroom-gated (free>=dbSize×1.25, CodeSafety refusal issues no restore). Opt-in POST /backups/restore-test?deep=true. Unit-tested via fake Runner with injectable seams (scratchRoot/diskFree/resolvePGBin). Reviewer-fixed 2 vacuous assertions + space-safe socket quoting.*** REMAINING 1.5: (a) DEEP end-to-end INTEGRATION test (needs a populated pgBackRest repo + real PG to prove restore+boot+count against real binaries — the one thing fake-Runner can't cover; integration-tagged, env-gated like readonly_integration_test.go; WON'T run in the loop's `go test ./...` gate by design). (c) the `digest` job is still unregistered, but NO digest builder exists yet — not load-bearing for 'never lose data', defer to band 2+. DONE iter 20: (b) DEEP UI opt-in button on Backups page (DeepRestoreTestConfirm; calls ?deep=true; verified_rows>0 'rows restored and verified' branch already renders). After (a), band 1.5 is complete → move to band 2 (stability). NOTE (a) is env-gated so it never proves green in the normal loop gate — pick it only when you can stand up a throwaway PG+pgBackRest stanza, else move to band 2. Optionally wire OTLP exporter (nil in collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue. Backups: internal/backup/manager.go (Backup takes backupMu.TryLock→ownership→terminal fail/success; RestoreTest verifyForRead→VerifyCmd→pass/fail row); internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups/ListRestoreTests newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup, InsertRestoreTest). Frontend SUCCESS_RESULTS={success,ok,completed,pass}/FAILURE_RESULTS={fail,failed,error} in Backups.tsx — Manager writes 'success'/'fail'. Scheduler: internal/scheduler — empty spec disables. go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
